Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible nephrotic-range protein loss and glycosuria not explained by hyperglycemia. Elevated ALT indicates mild liver involvement. Low serum globulin and low urine specific gravity further support renal dysfunction. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying renal pathology. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease with tubular involvement.
